verdict: PASS - reading.md is a faithful, well-scoped condensation of topic_corpus.md for 6.2. Six required sections present in order (Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References) with no YAML frontmatter -- first non-empty line is the H1 title. reading.meta.json exists and parses with topic_id, title, estimated_read_minutes, word_count, citations_used, sections. Independent whitespace-token count of the raw file (via offset/head_limit bisection) came to exactly 1967 tokens, matching the sidecar's self-reported word_count=1967 exactly, and sitting inside the deep-profile target_word_range [1800, 2200]; reading-aloud time at 230 wpm is ~8.55 min, inside the range implied by that band (the sidecar's estimated_read_minutes=10 uses a different, slower display formula -- a cosmetic discrepancy, not a length-budget fail, same pattern seen in 6.1's report). All three resources are cited with a matching, orphan-free bibliography: [1] docs.python.org (primary) is cited at least 10 times across Key Concepts and Worked Example, [2] Real Python and [3] DataCamp are each cited multiple times, and the References section lists exactly [1][2][3] with matching URLs and no orphan markers either direction. No forward references: read content/curriculum/sequence.index.json directly and confirmed 1.1-1.4 and 6.1 have populated concepts_introduced (status corpus_drafted), while 2.1 through 5.3 all have empty concepts_introduced arrays (status scaffolded, i.e. not yet built) -- 6.2 does not rely on any of them. Grepped every function signature (`def \w+\([^)]*=`) for default parameter values: zero matches -- every constructor and method in the reading uses only required positional parameters. Checked every 'if'/'else' occurrence: all three hits are the English conditional word used in prose ('if BaseA and BaseB both define...', 'if Base.__init__ sets self.__secret...') describing hypothetical scenarios in plain language, not Python ternary expressions or if/elif/else statements in code -- no actual conditional/ternary code appears anywhere in the reading. No for/while loops, lambda, yield, *args/**kwargs, or import statements found either. Faithfulness sampling (12 assertions spanning Overview/Key Concepts/Worked Example/In Practice/Key Takeaways -- the RegulatoryTask(Task) pass-only example, isinstance/issubclass/type() distinctions, the override-vs-extend framing, the three-level Person to Student to GraduateStudent super() chain, the gs.species class-attribute-lookup trace, the diamond MRO example and its 'super() means next-in-MRO not literal parent' lesson, single- vs double-underscore mangling and its collision-avoidance rationale, and the Key Takeaways) traced cleanly to topic_corpus.md with zero unsupported claims among the sampled set. One non-blocking observation, consistent with the 6.1 report: the Overview's closing italic sentence ('This contributes to A3 -- Python Foundations Diagnostic (due W8)...') has no basis anywhere in topic_corpus.md -- it is LMS/assessment scheduling metadata leaking into learner-facing prose, and is the one unsupported assertion found in the reading (well under the >2 fail threshold). Tone is plainspoken: zero hedging phrases found (searched for 'generally considered', 'many experts believe', 'various studies', 'arguably', 'tends to', etc. -- none present, threshold <3), and concrete worked code appears throughout Key Concepts (not deferred solely to the Worked Example section). Two paragraphs (lines 87 and 98, on attribute-lookup-through-a-chain and the diamond MRO trace respectively) run long and dense -- 6-7 sentences apiece -- at the outer edge of the wall-of-text guidance, but this mirrors the corpus's own necessarily-detailed MRO tracing and is not a scored violation.

checks.name: Format discipline
checks.detail: First non-empty line is '# Inheritance & Encapsulation' -- no YAML frontmatter block. reading.meta.json exists and parses, containing topic_id, title, estimated_read_minutes, word_count, generated_at, embeds_diagram, citations_used, sections. Exactly six '##' headings appear in the required order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References -- none added, renamed, dropped, or reordered.

checks.name: Length budget
checks.detail: Independent whitespace-token count of the full raw file (grep -o '\S+' bisected via offset/head_limit to find the final match index) totals exactly 1967 tokens, matching the sidecar's self-reported word_count=1967 precisely. Falls inside the deep-profile target_word_range [1800, 2200]. Reading-aloud estimate at 230 wpm = 1967/230 ~= 8.55 minutes, inside the range implied by that word band (~7.8-9.6 min). The sidecar's estimated_read_minutes=10 uses a different (slower) formula and is a display-only discrepancy, not a length-budget fail.

checks.name: Citations

[2] (Real Python) is cited on lines 49, 98, 118 (via [1][2][3] groupings), 156, 158, and again in the In Practice mixins bullet. [3] (DataCamp) is cited on lines 91, 93, 98, 118, 156, 158. References section lists exactly [1], [2], [3] with matching titles/URLs; no orphan inline markers and no orphan bibliography entries either direction.

checks.name: No forward references
checks.detail: Read content/curriculum/sequence.index.json directly: 6.2 lists prerequisite 6.1 (status corpus_drafted, populated concepts_introduced), and 1.1-1.4 are also status corpus_drafted with populated concepts_introduced. Topics 2.1 Conditionals, 2.2 Loops, 3.1 Functions, 3.2 Functional Constructs, 3.3 Modules, 4.1-4.2 Lists/Tuples, 5.1-5.3 Sets/Dictionaries/Iterators are all status='scaffolded' with empty concepts_introduced arrays -- confirmed not yet built, and 6.2 does not draw on any of them. Grepped every function signature ('def \\w+\\([^)]*=') for default parameter values: zero matches across the whole file -- every def in every code block (Task, RegulatoryTask, Person/Student/GraduateStudent, Base/Left/Right/Diamond, SecureAccount) uses only required positional parameters. Checked all 'if'/'else' occurrences: the 3 hits found are the English word 'if' used in plain prose describing hypothetical scenarios ('what happens if a class has more than one direct ancestor', 'if BaseA and BaseB both define the same method', 'if Base.__init__ sets self.__secret and a Derived subclass also sets self.__secret') -- none are Python conditional statements or ternary expressions in code. No for/while loops, lambda, yield, *args/**kwargs, list/dict/set literals, or import statements appear anywhere in the reading.

checks.name: Faithfulness to corpus
checks.detail: Sampled 12 assertions spanning all sections against topic_corpus.md: single-level inheritance and the immediately-usable pass-only RegulatoryTask(Task) (corpus 5.1), isinstance()/issubclass()/type() distinctions (5.1), overriding via method-lookup-order (5.1), the duplication problem motivating super() (5.1/5.2), super().__init__() resolving to the next class after the current one (5.2), the three-level Person -> Student -> GraduateStudent super() chain and its call-order/return-order trace (5.2), the gs.species class-attribute-lookup-after-instance-dict trace (5.2), the diamond problem / Diamond.__mro__ = (Diamond, Left, Right, Base, object) example and its 'super() means next-in-MRO, not literal parent' lesson (5.3), single-underscore-as-convention-only vs double-underscore name mangling to _ClassName__name (5.4), the collision-avoidance rationale via Base/Derived __secret example (5.4), the Worked Example's step-by-step RegulatoryTask build (Section 6), and the In Practice bullets on web frameworks/exception hierarchies/ORMs/mixins/published-library underscore conventions (Section 7) -- all traced verbatim or as faithful paraphrase, no new facts introduced. One unsupported assertion found: the Overview's closing sentence about contributing to 'A3 -- Python Foundations Diagnostic (due W8)' has no basis in topic_corpus.md (confirmed via direct grep for 'A3'/'Diagnostic'/'due W8' -- zero matches in the corpus); this is LMS scheduling metadata, not a taught concept, and 1 unsupported assertion is under the >2 fail threshold.

checks.name: Tone fit
checks.detail: Zero hedging phrases found ('it is generally considered', 'many experts believe', 'various studies have shown', 'arguably', 'tends to', 'somewhat', and variants all absent; threshold <3). Concrete worked code appears inside Key Concepts itself (Task/RegulatoryTask, Person/Student/GraduateStudent, Base/Left/Right/Diamond, SecureAccount/Base/Derived), not deferred entirely to the Worked Example section. Bolded terms are defined in plain language at first use (subclass, superclass, overriding, extending, Method Resolution Order, diamond problem, encapsulation, name mangling). Most paragraphs run 3-5 sentences; two (lines 87 and 98, tracing multi-level attribute lookup and the diamond MRO call chain) run to 6-7 sentences -- dense but proportionate to the inherently multi-step mechanics being traced, not hedging or jargon-dumping, and not a scored violation.
